message:
fix(ft-optim): guard StepLR and CyclicLR against zero-period panics

`StepLR::new(.., step_size)` and `CyclicLR::new(.., step_size_up)` (plus
`CyclicLR::step_size_down(size)`) accepted a literal `0` from callers and
then panicked on the very first `.step()` call:

- `StepLR::compute_lr_at_epoch` performs the integer division
  `epoch / step_size`, which traps on `step_size == 0`.
- `CyclicLR::compute_lr` performs both an integer division
  `iteration / total_size` and a float division
  `iteration as f64 / step_size_up as f64`. The integer path traps;
  the float path silently produces `inf` and poisons the rest of the
  triangular-wave formula.

Collapse a zero period to the smallest valid period (`1`, i.e. decay
or oscillate every iteration) using the same defensive `.max(1)` guard
that `CosineAnnealingLR`, `OneCycleLR`, and
`CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts` already use for their analogous
periodicity arguments. Apply the guard in three places in
`crates/ft-optim/src/lib.rs`:

1. `StepLR::new`'s `step_size` field initialization.
2. `CyclicLR::new`'s `step_size_up` local before it is captured.
3. `CyclicLR::step_size_down` setter, so a chained
   `.step_size_down(0)` after a valid `new` cannot drop
   `total_size = step_size_up + step_size_down` to zero.

A zero period has no valid scheduler interpretation, so collapsing to
`1` is the safest non-panicking behavior consistent with the rest of
the scheduler family. Each guard carries an inline comment naming the
exact division site that would otherwise panic, so a future reader
can recognize the precondition without rederiving it from the
triangular-wave math.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
